description_long: This object is a historical cannon mounted on a wooden carriage, reminiscent of artillery used in the 18th and 19th centuries. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and made of dark, weathered metal, featuring subtle reinforcement rings along its length. The carriage is constructed from heavy, reddish-brown wood, reinforced with metal brackets and bolts, and is supported by four solid wooden wheels. The wheels are simple, with visible axles and metal fastenings, designed to allow the cannon to be repositioned. The entire assembly rests on a flat, rectangular base, likely to stabilize the cannon during firing. The surface of the cannon and carriage shows signs of age and use, with a rough, matte finish and some visible wear.
description: A historical cannon with a long, dark metal barrel mounted on a sturdy wooden carriage with four wheels and metal reinforcements. The object has a weathered, matte appearance and sits on a flat base.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the cannon barrel and the detailed wooden carriage with four wheels.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular muzzle of the cannon and the alignment of the wheels beneath the barrel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the other side of the carriage and barrel details.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the carriage, the breech of the cannon, and the wheel arrangement.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on a wooden carriage.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; surfaces are solid dark and reddish-brown colors.
